meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement a professional client workflow and overcome the no portfolio trap. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building fake clones. Instead, document a client-facing workflow that delivers a tangible asset. By using Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform a conceptual project into a professional case study that proves your capability to real employers.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is the silent killer of entry-level engineering careers. You have the skills, you have the passion, but you lack the one thing recruiters demand: evidence of real-world impact. The frustration of building 'To-Do' apps that never see production is a rite of passage, but it's time to graduate from tutorials to outcomes.

This guide shifts your strategy from 'coding exercises' to 'client-ready workflows.' By leveraging the rapid prototyping power of Antigravity alongside the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you will build a project that isn't just code—it's a solution. You aren't just learning to code; you are learning to ship.

steps.title: Define the Client Problem
steps.description: Identify a small business owner's manual bottleneck. A portfolio piece is only valuable if it solves a business constraint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior consultant. Define a 3-page scope for an automated lead-capture system for a local coffee shop. Focus on database schema and user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the client problem, proposed solution, and tech stack using Next.js and Supabase.'

steps.title: Architect the Database
steps.description: Structure your data before writing logic. This demonstrates professional planning skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the SQL schema for a lead capture system, including tables for users, leads, and engagement logs. Ensure strict typing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the SQL schema to the Supabase migration file and initialize the database connection.'

steps.title: Build the Core Workflow
steps.description: Implement the core logic that moves data from the UI to the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust server-side action to handle form submissions, validate inputs, and store them securely in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the form submission handler in the API route and add error handling for invalid user inputs.'

steps.title: Integrate UI Components
steps.description: Use modern design systems to ensure your app looks enterprise-grade from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive landing page component using Tailwind CSS that emphasizes the lead capture call-to-action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the main landing page component, integrating the form logic from the previous step.'

steps.title: Document the Case Study
steps.description: Turn the project into a story. Explain the 'why' behind your technical decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a case study summary for a portfolio site. Include the problem, the technical implementation, and the performance results.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a file named CASE_STUDY.md that details the project journey and technical challenges solved.'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'business outcome' in your README. Recruiters care more about why you chose a tool than the tool itself.
Use git commits as a timeline of your problem-solving process; it shows you understand professional version control.
Deploy your MVP immediately. A live link is worth 10,000 lines of local code.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your documentation to show you think about scalability.
Never hide your failures. Document a bug you encountered and how you used Claude Code to debug it.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, deploy it to Vercel, link the repository to your LinkedIn profile, and reach out to one local business to offer a free audit of their current web presence.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Build, ship, and document your way to a career that pays for your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
